hero.title: Panic Delivery Wiki
hero.description: Team up as disposable couriers, deliver packages through monster-run dystopian zones, manage shared lives, survive missions, unlock cosmetics, and learn maps, enemies, items, and package rules.

gameFeature.title: Panic Delivery Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Team up as disposable couriers in a 4-player co-op horror comedy, deliver and smuggle packages through monster-controlled zones, manage 99 shared lives, and unlock cosmetics across procedurally generated maps on Steam.

releaseDateEarlyAccess.title: Panic Delivery Release Date and Early Access
releaseDateEarlyAccess.intro: Panic Delivery launched into Steam Early Access on May 13, 2026 as a 4-player co-op horror comedy about couriers delivering, recovering, and smuggling packages through monster-controlled locations while sharing a limited pool of 99 lives.

items.label: Early Access Launch
items.details: The Steam Early Access version is live now for PC players.

items.label: Platform
items.details: Steam lists Panic Delivery with Single-player and Online Co-op support.

items.label: Base Price
items.details: The Early Access game is sold on Steam as a paid release. The developer notes the price may increase slightly after Early Access.

items.label: Early Access Window
items.details: The developer plans to use the Early Access period to expand content, refine systems, and improve the game with player feedback.

items.label: Current Build Content
items.details: The launch build includes procedurally generated dungeons, Standard, Recovery, and Smuggling missions, 13 enemies, 15 shop items, character customization, 3 package types, and a challenge system for cosmetics.

items.label: Supported Languages
items.details: Steam lists English, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Japanese, Portuguese - Brazil, Russian, and Simplified Chinese. English includes full audio.

beginnerGuide.title: Panic Delivery Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: New players should focus on the core loop before chasing cosmetics or harder missions. Panic Delivery is built around repeat runs, shared lives, mission rewards, shop upgrades, and learning how each monster-controlled route behaves.

steps.title: Start as a Courier
steps.description: Enter a run solo or with online co-op teammates. Your team plays as couriers sent into a dystopian world ruled by monsters.

steps.title: Check the Mission Type
steps.description: Early Access includes Standard, Recovery, and Smuggling missions. Each mission type uses randomized objectives, so check the run goal before rushing into danger.

steps.title: Handle the Package
steps.description: The game includes Small, Medium, and Large package types. Your objective is tied to delivering, recovering, or smuggling packages through the active route.

steps.title: Protect the Shared 99 Lives
steps.description: The team shares 99 backup clones. Each death deploys another clone, but running out of lives resets the run.

steps.title: Earn and Spend Mission Rewards
steps.description: Missions reward Speetchy, which can be spent on weapons and tools in the shop. Bonus Permits are used for cosmetic swag.

steps.title: Keep Permanent Purchases
steps.description: When the team runs out of lives, the run resets, but purchased swag is kept. This makes early upgrades and cosmetics part of long-term progress.

quickTips: Read the mission objective before moving so the team commits to the right package goal.
Treat the shared 99 lives as a team resource, not a personal health bar.
Save Speetchy for tools that protect the package carrier or speed up the route.
Coordinate movement so the carrier is never left alone during monster pressure.

coopMultiplayerGuide.title: Panic Delivery Co-op Multiplayer Guide
coopMultiplayerGuide.intro: The multiplayer loop is built around coordination: complete the delivery objective, manage the shared clone pool, avoid enemy pressure, and return with rewards. Panic Delivery is not only about moving fast; it is about spending the team's limited lives wisely.

items.title: Player Count
items.value: Solo or up to 4 players online
items.description: Steam lists both Single-player and Online Co-op support, and the game is described as a 4-player co-op horror comedy.

items.title: Shared Lives
items.description: Couriers share a pool of 99 backup clones. Deaths reduce the team's remaining chances, so reckless play burns progress for everyone.

items.title: Respawn Loop
items.description: A dead courier can continue through the cloning system, but the pool is limited. Once the team runs out of lives, the run resets.

items.title: Team Objective
items.description: Co-op teams need to complete the current mission objective while dealing with randomized goals, procedural dungeons, and enemy encounters.

items.title: Route Awareness
items.description: The Early Access build includes three maps with procedurally generated dungeons, creating changing routes instead of one fixed delivery path.

items.title: Co-op Priority
items.description: The safest team is the one that tracks the objective, watches the shared lives, and avoids splitting up at the worst possible moment.

missionsPackageTypes.title: Panic Delivery Missions and Package Types
missionsPackageTypes.intro: Panic Delivery missions are built around package work in dangerous, procedurally generated locations. Before starting a run, players should identify the mission type, package size, route risk, and escape plan.

rows.gameData: One of the three Early Access mission types with randomized objectives.
rows.checklist: Confirm the delivery objective, move the assigned package, avoid unnecessary deaths, and return with the team's lives intact.

rows.gameData: One of the three Early Access mission types with randomized objectives.
rows.checklist: Find the recovery target, secure the package or item tied to the objective, and leave before the shared clone pool gets drained.

rows.gameData: One of the three Early Access mission types with randomized objectives.
rows.checklist: Track the smuggling objective, protect the package, coordinate movement, and avoid turning the route into a group mistake.

rows.gameData: One of the three delivery package types included in the Early Access build.
rows.checklist: Assign a courier to the package, keep the route clear, and prioritize safe completion over exploration.

rows.gameData: One of the three delivery package types included in the Early Access build.
rows.checklist: Plan the route around the objective, keep teammates close enough to help, and watch the shared lives during enemy pressure.

rows.gameData: One of the three delivery package types included in the Early Access build.
rows.checklist: Treat the package as a team task, reduce risky detours, and make sure the carrier is not abandoned.

rows.gameData: Standard, Recovery, and Smuggling missions use randomized objectives.
rows.checklist: Check the objective at the start of every run instead of assuming the previous route plan still works.

rows.gameData: The launch Early Access version includes 3 maps, each with procedurally generated dungeons.
rows.checklist: Expect changing layouts, confirm the objective path, and adapt before the monsters adapt for you.

mapsAndZonesGuide.title: Panic Delivery Maps and Zones Guide
mapsAndZonesGuide.intro: Panic Delivery launches with 3 maps, each built around procedurally generated dungeon-style layouts. This guide helps couriers understand the major zone themes, the mission types that can appear, and the risks that affect delivery speed, cargo safety, and the shared 99 lives pool.

cards.name: Early Access Map Pool
gameplayData: 3 Early Access maps
Procedurally generated dungeons on each map
3 difficulty modes
Delivery, Recovery, and Smuggling mission types
risks: Lost progress when the team runs out of shared lives
cards.routeTip: Assign one courier to carry the package, one to scout the next room, and one to keep a survival tool ready before the team commits to a route.

cards.name: Haunted Route
cards.routeFocus: Tense interior paths, horror-heavy rooms, and sudden monster pressure
gameplayData: Part of the deadly location set shown for Early Access
Used for high-risk package runs
Built for replayable procedural routes
cards.routeTip: Keep the package carrier in the middle of the group and avoid splitting up unless the objective forces a search pattern.

cards.name: Panic Factory
cards.routeFocus: Factory paths tied to the human-cloning workplace theme
gameplayData: Listed as a major deadly location
Connected to the cloning and corporate horror setting
Works with randomized mission objectives
risks: Enemies interrupting item use or package handling
cards.routeTip: Use the factory run as a coordination check: call out terminals, keep exits marked, and do not let the package carrier handle every interaction.

cards.name: Arctic Base
cards.routeFocus: An icy base route built around alien-secret atmosphere and exploration hazards
gameplayData: Known Early Access map zone
Hotfix 0.1.1 removed invisible barriers that blocked exploration in some Arctic Base areas
Procedural layouts keep routes from repeating cleanly
cards.routeTip: Treat Arctic Base routes as exploration-heavy: check side paths before moving the package deep into the map, then regroup before the final delivery push.

cards.name: Mission Route Rules
cards.routeFocus: Standard delivery, recovery work, and smuggling assignments
gameplayData: Delivery missions move packages through hazardous routes
Recovery missions use tracking devices to locate lost cargo
Smuggling missions involve special packages and resistance-linked objectives
risks: Higher rewards tied to harder routes
cards.routeTip: Pick items around the mission type: recovery needs tracking utility, delivery needs protection around the carrier, and smuggling needs a clean exit plan.

monstersAndEnemiesGuide.title: Panic Delivery Monsters and Enemies Guide
monstersAndEnemiesGuide.intro: Panic Delivery launches with 13 unique enemies in a monster-filled dystopia. Enemy handling is less about memorizing a perfect route and more about protecting the package carrier, spending tools wisely, and knowing when to fight, flee, or regroup before the shared life pool collapses.

cards.name: Early Access Enemy Pool
gameplayData: 13 unique enemies in the Early Access build
Enemies appear across procedural maps and mission routes
Additional map-specific enemies are part of the Early Access content roadmap
cards.behavior: Enemy pressure changes with route layout, objective placement, and team noise during co-op movement.
cards.counterplay: Enter rooms together, keep the package carrier protected, and save defensive tools for the return path.

cards.name: Lootmuncher
gameplayData: Hotfix 0.1.3 improved Lootmuncher escape behavior
The update made its movement more consistent during escape behavior
cards.behavior: Lootmuncher is important enough to receive a named behavior fix during Early Access.
cards.counterplay: Watch its movement when it disengages or flees, then use the moment to reposition, recover dropped items, or move the package.

cards.name: Grotesque Monsters
gameplayData: The game world is ruled by grotesque monsters
Deliveries take place through a monster-infested dystopia
Monsters create the main pressure during delivery, recovery, and smuggling missions
cards.behavior: These threats punish slow routing, separated teammates, and messy package handling.
cards.counterplay: One courier calls the route, one protects the carrier, and the rest move.

cards.name: Map-Specific Threats
gameplayData: Future Early Access updates are planned to add more map-specific enemies
New enemy behaviors are intended to expand gameplay variety
Each map uses procedural dungeon layouts for repeatable but unpredictable encounters
cards.behavior: Zone identity matters because a factory route, haunted route, and ice-base route create different movement and visibility problems.
cards.counterplay: Build item loadouts around the zone: defensive tools for tight interiors, traversal tools for long routes, and repellents for exits.

cards.name: Chase Pressure
gameplayData: Players can fend off hostile monsters or outrun them
Weapons, gadgets, traversal tools, and monster repellents support survival
Sometimes running is the better option
cards.behavior: Enemies force quick decisions during package transport, especially when the team is carrying fragile or high-risk cargo.
cards.counterplay: Fight only when it opens a route. If fighting turns into a hallway standoff, leave, because survival still beats workplace pride.

itemsTierList.title: Panic Delivery Items Tier List
itemsTierList.intro: The Early Access build includes 15 shop items. Missions award Speetchy, which can be spent on weapons, tools, defensive gear, utility options, and swag, while Bonus Permits are used for cosmetic-style purchases. The best early buys are the ones that protect the package carrier, recover lost cargo, or help the squad escape before the 99 lives system runs dry.

items.name: Tracking Devices
items.bestFor: Recovery missions and scattered cargo searches
items.why: Recovery missions rely on tracking devices to retrieve lost cargo across dangerous terrain, making this one of the most mission-critical utility categories.
items.teamUse: Give tracking responsibility to one courier while the rest protect the route and package path.

items.name: Crowbar
items.bestFor: Breaking boxes, emergency defense, and direct route control
items.why: The crowbar combines practical route interaction with close-range protection when monsters pressure the team.
items.teamUse: Keep it near the front scout or the courier guarding the package carrier.

items.name: Monster Repellents
items.bestFor: Escape routes, panic recoveries, and protecting the package carrier
items.why: Repellents support survival when fighting slows the team down, especially during the final route back or a bad procedural layout.
items.teamUse: Save repellents for exits, revives, or moments when the carrier cannot afford to drop cargo.

items.name: Traversal Tools
items.bestFor: Long routes, procedural rooms, and risky return paths
items.why: Procedural maps reward faster repositioning, and traversal tools help teams adapt when objectives spawn across awkward routes.
items.teamUse: Use mobility tools to scout, reconnect separated couriers, or create a safer path for the package carrier.

items.name: Gadgets
items.bestFor: Situational support across delivery, recovery, and smuggling missions
items.why: Gadgets can support different playstyles, but their value depends heavily on map layout and mission type.
items.teamUse: Buy gadgets after the team already has one strong defensive option and one route-control tool.

items.name: Groan Tube
items.bestFor: Short-range panic defense and chaotic co-op moments
items.why: It works as a practical backup weapon, though it should not replace serious utility when the mission requires clean routing.
items.teamUse: Use it as emergency protection, not as a reason for four couriers to stand still.

items.name: Smuggling Tools
items.bestFor: Smuggling assignments and resistance-linked package objectives
items.why: Smuggling missions involve special packages with unique tools and items, so these tools matter most when that mission type is active.
items.teamUse: Assign one courier to handle smuggling-specific tools while another keeps the exit route clear.

items.name: Boujee Swag
items.bestFor: Persistent personal style and non-survival purchases
items.why: Purchased swag remains valuable for personalization, but it does not outperform survival tools during dangerous delivery routes.
items.teamUse: Buy swag after the team has enough tools to complete runs consistently.

updatesAndPatchNotes.title: Panic Delivery Updates and Patch Notes
updatesAndPatchNotes.intro: Panic Delivery is in Steam Early Access, so updates focus on stability, mission pacing, map fixes, enemy behavior, audio, and new content around the launch window. This timeline gives players the practical version: what changed, when it changed, and why it matters in a run.

entries.title: Original Soundtrack Release
highlights: Panic Delivery Original Soundtrack released on Steam
47 tracks from the game
Composed and produced by Jonah Buhat Gardose
Includes Trap, Dubstep, Techno, Chiptune, and orchestral-style tracks
entries.impact: Adds an official soundtrack option for players who want the game's corporate chaos outside active deliveries.

entries.title: Hotfix Patch 0.1.3
highlights: Improved Lootmuncher behavior when escaping
Fixed a critical crash caused by a player pushing another player by running into them
Prevented players from being moved by others while interacting with terminals
entries.impact: Improves enemy consistency and reduces co-op interaction problems around terminals.

entries.title: Hotfix Patch 0.1.2
highlights: Added new music tracks to Panic Truck Radio
Reduced the Resources required to open the Smuggling Area
Improved the overall audio mix
Improved Resource Terminal feedback
Fixed gamepad Push-To-Talk input
Fixed an issue that could leave a player stuck inside a wall
entries.impact: Makes smuggling progression smoother, improves audio readability, and removes several practical friction points.

entries.title: Panic Delivery + MAZEBOUND Bundle
highlights: Panic Delivery was bundled with MAZEBOUND on Steam
The announcement included a Discord key giveaway for MAZEBOUND
The bundle targets multiplayer survival fans
entries.impact: Gives co-op players a discounted bundle path around the Early Access launch period.

entries.title: Hotfix Patch 0.1.1
highlights: Added an FPS limit setting
Removed unintended invisible barriers in the Arctic Base map
Fixed minor client-side replication issues
Improved multiplayer synchronization and gameplay interaction consistency
entries.impact: Improves performance control, Arctic Base exploration, and online co-op stability.

entries.title: Steam Early Access Launch
highlights: Panic Delivery launched in Early Access on Steam
4-player co-op horror comedy delivery gameplay
Shared pool of 99 company-sponsored lives
Launch price listed at $7.99 USD with a 10% Early Access release discount at $7.19 USD
Official soundtrack was announced for release after launch
entries.impact: Opened the core Early Access version with co-op delivery, recovery, smuggling, procedural maps, enemies, shop items, and the shared-lives system.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Panic Delivery

questions.question: What is Panic Delivery?
questions.answer: Panic Delivery is a 4-player co-op horror comedy courier survival game on Steam. You play as disposable couriers delivering, recovering, and smuggling packages through monster-controlled dystopian locations while sharing a pool of 99 lives.

questions.question: When did Panic Delivery release?
questions.answer: Panic Delivery launched into Steam Early Access on May 13, 2026 for PC. The developer plans to keep it in Early Access for approximately one year to expand content with player feedback.

questions.question: How much does Panic Delivery cost?
questions.answer: Panic Delivery is listed at $7.99 USD on Steam, with a 10% Early Access release discount that brought the launch price to $7.19 USD. The developer notes the price may increase slightly after Early Access.

questions.question: Does Panic Delivery support co-op multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Panic Delivery supports both single-player and online co-op for up to four players. Co-op teams share the pool of 99 backup clone lives, so coordination matters as much as speed.

questions.question: What platforms is Panic Delivery available on?
questions.answer: Panic Delivery is available on PC via Steam. The Early Access launch build includes 3 maps, 3 difficulty modes, 3 mission types, 13 enemies, and 15 shop items.

questions.question: Is Panic Delivery a finished game?
questions.answer: Panic Delivery is an Early Access title, so it is still expanding rather than finished. The current build is fully playable, and the developer plans to add more maps, enemies, items, missions, and delivery options during the Early Access period.
